step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the World Model and transcript, the architecture represents Levels Beyond's Media to Cloud workflow. We define nodes for all entities specified in the World Model (including the User, storage services, step functions, serverless processing services, database, and Reach Engine container compute). The flow represents a unified ingestion and media enrichment workflow, which begins with the User uploading files, sequentially triggers transcoding, celebrity/visual extraction, audio transcription, NLP analysis, and temporary DynamoDB storage, before finally ingesting into the Reach Engine on EKS.

graph.name: Levels Beyond Media to Cloud Enrichment Workflow
graph.notes: An AWS Step Functions orchestrated serverless workflow for ingesting media into Reach Engine. Videos are transcoded to browser-compatible proxies via AWS Elemental MediaConvert, while parallel metadata extraction is handled via Amazon Rekognition, Transcribe, and Comprehend before storing technical and enriched metadata in Amazon DynamoDB.

nodes.name: User
nodes.notes: Represents the user uploading media files to the ingest bucket.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: S3 bucket where the raw media files are uploaded and proxy files are stored.

nodes.name: Step Functions
nodes.notes: Orchestrates the media processing workflows, triggering encoding and metadata extraction services.

nodes.name: Elemental MediaConvert
nodes.notes: Transcodes input videos into browser-compatible proxies.

nodes.name: Rekognition
nodes.notes: Analyzes visual media for celebrity recognition, content moderation, and other visual tags.

nodes.name: Transcribe
nodes.notes: Translates the audio from the video into text.

nodes.name: Comprehend
nodes.notes: Performs natural language processing on transcribed text to extract insights and entities.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Serves as a temporary database storing the extracted metadata from multiple streams.

nodes.name: EC2 (Reach Engine)
nodes.notes: Computes part of the Reach Engine system.

nodes.name: EKS (Reach Engine)
nodes.notes: Runs the containerized Reach Engine orchestrator and platform.

edges.notes: User uploads the raw video file into the S3 ingest bucket.
Upload event triggers the AWS Step Functions workflow orchestration.
Step Functions initiates the video transcoding job in Elemental MediaConvert.
Elemental MediaConvert saves the generated proxy video back to the S3 bucket.
Step Functions triggers AWS Rekognition to extract visual and tag data from the video.
Visual analysis results and tags are stored in DynamoDB.
Step Functions triggers Amazon Transcribe to convert audio to text.
Transcribed text is sent to Amazon Comprehend for entity and sentiment analysis.
Comprehend text analytics results are saved in DynamoDB.
Extracted metadata in DynamoDB is imported into the Reach Engine platform running on EKS for user search and curation.
